items.headline: Study session on zoning and parkland protections
items.summary: City Council will consider existing designations, zoning corrections, and state law protections for recreational facilities and parkland sites. The agenda does not specify which sites or what corrections are under review.

items.headline: Two new single-family homes permitted on Chamberlain Drive
items.summary: Permits issued for two new 2-story houses at 3550 and 3554 Chamberlain Dr, totaling approximately $1.09 million.

items.headline: Fifth Third Bank permitted to install illuminated signs at Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The bank received approval to add three illuminated LED wall signs at 250 Lytton Avenue, marking a new financial services tenant in downtown Palo Alto.

items.headline: City to study eliminating chemical pesticides on public property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission is reviewing a policy to ban chemical pesticide use on city-owned or leased land. The outcome could affect grounds maintenance at parks, facilities, and other municipal properties.

items.headline: Artificial turf policy under review by Sustainability Commission
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission is studying the city's use of artificial turf, likely examining environmental and health impacts. Any policy change could influence future landscaping decisions at parks and public spaces.
